message:
fix(opencode): use unique synthetic source_path per session

The OpenCode connector set source_path to the bare DB path
(opencode.db) for ALL sessions, making them indistinguishable.
This broke the cass sessions→export round-trip: export couldn't
resolve which session to extract from the DB path alone.

Now follows the Cursor connector pattern: appends the URL-encoded
session ID to create unique synthetic paths like
opencode.db/ses_abc123. These non-existent paths cause the export
command to use the indexed conversation lookup path, which works
correctly.

Users need to run `cass index --full` after updating to re-scan
OpenCode sessions with the new synthetic paths.

Closes Dicklesworthstone/coding_agent_session_search#149

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -55,11 +55,13 @@ chatgpt = ["connectors", "dep:aes-gcm", "dep:base64"]
 # Cursor: SQLite database access for state.vscdb
 cursor = ["connectors", "dep:rusqlite", "dep:urlencoding"]
 # OpenCode: SQLite database access for v1.2+ opencode.db
-opencode = ["connectors", "dep:rusqlite"]
+opencode = ["connectors", "dep:rusqlite", "dep:urlencoding"]
+# Goose: SQLite database access for Goose sessions.db
+goose = ["connectors", "dep:rusqlite"]
 # Crush: SQLite database access for Charm's Crush agent (uses frankensqlite)
 crush = ["connectors", "dep:frankensqlite"]
 # All connectors enabled
-all-connectors = ["connectors", "chatgpt", "cursor", "opencode", "crush"]
+all-connectors = ["connectors", "chatgpt", "cursor", "opencode", "goose", "crush"]
 
 [dev-dependencies]
 tempfile = "3"

@@ -194,7 +194,7 @@ impl OpenCodeConnector {
                 external_id: Some(session.id.clone()),
                 title,
                 workspace,
-                source_path: db_path.to_path_buf(),
+                source_path: db_path.join(urlencoding::encode(&session.id).as_ref()),
                 started_at,
                 ended_at,
                 metadata: serde_json::json!({
